title: robustly roasted greek lamb spanakopita
description: a crumbly, kid-friendly greek dish featuring lamb shoulder, wrapped in a flaky phyllo pastry, and flavored with a hint of feta cheese and fresh dill.
instructions: preheat the oven to 375 degrees fahrenheit.
in a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium-high heat.
add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
remove the lamb from the skillet and set aside.
in the same skillet, add the chopped dill, crumbled feta cheese, chopped spinach, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper.
cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the spinach has wilted.
stir in the cooked lamb and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
unroll the phyllo dough and cut it into 4 equal pieces.
place one piece of phyllo dough on a flat surface and brush with olive oil.
place half of the lamb and spinach mixture on one half of the phyllo dough.
fold the phyllo dough in half to enclose the filling.
repeat with the remaining phyllo dough and lamb mixture.
place the spanakopitas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown.
